steps.action: Inspect openapi.json for setup-facing item and packaging paths plus retired setup hosts
steps.expected: OpenAPI keeps `/api/v1/catalog/items` and `/api/v1/factory/packaging-mappings`, and does not advertise `/api/v1/catalog/products` or `/api/v1/accounting/catalog/products` as setup hosts.
steps.observed: `openapi_catalog_item_contract.json` shows `/api/v1/catalog/items` with GET/POST, `/api/v1/catalog/items/{itemId}` with GET/PUT/DELETE, `/api/v1/factory/packaging-mappings` with GET/POST, and both retired setup hosts absent.

steps.expected: Setup-facing docs/handoffs point stock-bearing setup to catalog items and packaging mappings without retired product hosts.
steps.observed: The docs summary file records repeated `/api/v1/catalog/items` references in all requested setup docs, packaging-mappings references in endpoint inventory/frontend handoff, and zero matches for `/api/v1/catalog/products` or `/api/v1/accounting/catalog/products` in those setup-facing files.

steps.action: Review surviving `production-batches` mentions in frontend handoff for context
steps.expected: `/api/v1/factory/production-batches` may remain execution-only in this milestone but must not be described as setup.
steps.observed: The surviving `/api/v1/factory/production-batches` rows sit in the factory execution section of `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md`, not in the catalog/setup guidance that now points to `/api/v1/catalog/items`.

assertions.title: Single-item setup hides internal family ids

steps.expected: The public setup contract is single-item entry on `/api/v1/catalog/items` and does not require raw family/group identifiers.
steps.observed: `CatalogItemRequest` requires only `brandId`, `gstRate`, `hsnCode`, `itemClass`, `name`, and `unitOfMeasure`; its properties contain no `familyId`, `groupId`, or `variantGroupId`. `CatalogItemDto` also omits those raw grouping fields.

steps.action: Inspect setup-facing handoff guidance for Product Family wording
steps.expected: If Product Family is mentioned, it is explanatory vocabulary only and not an operator input requirement.
steps.observed: `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md` states that UI may label grouped sellable sizes as `Product Family`, but the public setup contract does not ask operators for raw family/group identifiers and no public `familyId`, `groupId`, or `variantGroupId` field is required.

steps.expected: Docs describe single-item create/update on `/api/v1/catalog/items` rather than the retired product host.
steps.observed: README plus accounting/frontend handoff docs consistently describe brand creation followed by single-item create/update on `/api/v1/catalog/items` and `/api/v1/catalog/items/{itemId}`.

assertions.title: Item readiness is visible before factory execution

steps.expected: Catalog list/detail responses can include readiness information.
steps.observed: `CatalogItemDto` carries a `readiness` property referencing `SkuReadinessDto`, and both catalog list/detail GET operations document `includeReadiness` query parameters.

steps.expected: The runtime list contract returns readiness snapshots before execution.
steps.observed: Login returned 200; the catalog list returned 200 with 4 items, and each row included readiness stages keyed by `masterReady`, `inventoryReady`, `productionReady`, `packingReady`, `salesReady`, and `accountingReady`.

steps.expected: Item detail also exposes readiness for a specific item.
steps.observed: Returned 200 for `Fixture Finished Good` with readiness `sku=FG-FIXTURE`; `packingReady` was false with blocker `PACKAGING_SIZE_MISSING`, proving setup/factory users can see why the item is not execution-ready yet.

steps.expected: Regression tests cover computed readiness on create/list/detail paths.
